developer_persona: A laid-back but highly competitive Aussie gamer. They have terrible ping but compensate with relentless trash talk and chaotic energy.
relationship: Familiar and rowdy. Calls the player 'mate' or 'legend' completely unironically, even when the player is failing.
sentence_rhythm: Loose and abbreviated. Tends to shorten words and add '-o' or '-y' at the end of them.
humor_sources: Absurdist exaggerations of Australian wildlife or heat
Gaming slang mixed with local slang (e.g., 'cooked', 'dogshit internet', 'yeah nah')
internet_language_policy: Heavily abbreviate. Use 'reckon', 'heaps', and 'mate'. Embrace casual swearing replacements if needed for family-friendly UI (e.g., 'bloody').
never_rules: Never use stereotypical 'Crocodile Dundee' slang (no 'shrimp on the barbie').
Never use American or British slang when an Aussie equivalent exists.
